translated: But if I see that you are not doing well or you are not being serious and disciplined, then I will withdraw my support.

text: Jekob yaa pai niak tiib ale almond tiib ale dapeelita tiib naŋ yɔgsisa ate ba che la a po, a yaa pai daataŋa ate wa poa ate ŋa peenti la a dueni peelim.
translated: Jacob took fresh branches from poplar and almond and plane trees and peeled white stripes in them, exposing the white of the branches.

translated: If you do these things, "I will not give you rain in its season and the earth will not yield its produce, and I will send wild animals among you."

ref: Before Rachel left, she stole the household idols while Laban was out shearing his sheep.

translated: When Laban heard that his daughter had been taken to a distant land, Arekel went to his house in the evening.

ref: I could easily hurt you, but the God your father worshiped has warned me not to make any threats or promises.
text: M ta pagrim a baga a nye fu wari kama. Alege yokku po, fi kowa Wenni a weeni mu kama ayen. Be seba ate fi kan weeni biem wari yaase nalim wari a te Jekob.
translated: I will not forget you or abandon you like money. But today, you should know that God has shown me something. They say you can know people's money through their children's money like Jacob.

ref: Rachel said, “Father, please don't be angry with me for not getting up; I'm having my period.” Laban kept on searching, but still did not find the idols.
text: Arekel yaa weeni wa kowa ayen, <<M nyɔnɔ, kan te fi sui a puuri ale mu m ni kan baga yiti a zaani fi niŋ la. M boka m chiak lɔka po.>> Ate wa gisi alege wa an baga a nya yenni wenaŋa.
translated: The man said to his wife, "My friend, if you don't stop this foolishness, I will divorce you and marry another woman. I am tired of your behavior." And he left her like that and went to marry another woman.

ref: When the messengers returned, they told Jacob, “We went to your brother Esau, and now he is heading this way with
text: Tintomdɔma de ale ŋman cheŋ Jekob jigi la, ba weeni wa kama ayen. Ti cheŋ fi suoku Iisɔ jigi kama ate wa cheena ale nuruba kobsinaansi ayen ba tu fu suik.
translated: The scripture that we read about Jacob's life, they did not write it for nothing. The things that happened to Jacob are written so that we too should learn from them and not do the same.

ref: After Jacob had spent the night there, he chose some animals as gifts for Esau:

translated: If you do not remain in me, and if you do not eat my flesh, you will not have life and the thing will not be good for you to receive Jesus.

ref: Then he said to the servant in charge of the first herd, “When Esau meets you, he will ask whose servant you are. He will want to know where you are going and who owns those animals in front of you.

translated: And when they saw him they were amazed, and his mother said to him, "Son, why have you treated us so? Behold, your father and I have been searching for you in great distress."

translated: The reason why the Israelites do not eat the tendon attached to the socket of the hip to this day is because the socket of Jacob's hip was touched at the tendon.

ref: So tell him, ‘They belong to your servant Jacob, who is coming this way. He is sending them as a gift to his master Esau.’ ”
text: Fi yaa weeni wa ayen, <Ka fi tomteerɔwa Jekob ale soa. Ŋa ka tiirim ate wa pai tom ayen m ta jam te m nyɔnɔwa Iisɔ, wa abe bo ka ti ŋaaŋ a cheena.> >>
translated: You will see that, <It is not by the works of Jacob that he was saved. But it is by faith in the word that I have preached that I have received Jesus, who has come to give us his grace.>

ref: Then the man asked, “What is your name?” “Jacob,” he answered.
text: Nuruwa yaa bek wa ayen, <<Fi yuei ale boa?>> Wa yaa tulisi ayen, <<Jekob.>>

text: Dila nyiŋ a jam paari jinla Izirali demma a kan ŋɔbi lam buui ale ka jaab kɔsuk la, diila Jekob kɔsukku ate wa jam tiri la nyiŋ.

translated: The dream that he dreamed in his father's house, he remembered Jacob's promise and his promise to build a house and they would worship him there.

ref: Jacob said, “Now tell me your name.” “Don't you know who I am?” he asked. And he blessed Jacob.
text: Jekob yaa weeni wa ayen, <<Mi a saalim fu, wi fu yuei a sak mu.>> Alege wa tulisi wa ayen, <<Ka boan nyiŋ ate fi a bek mi yuei ya?>> Ate wa yaa niak wa dula.
translated: Jacob said to him, "I will not let you go unless you bless me." Then he asked him, "What is your name?" And he said, "Jacob."

translated: These are the names of Esau's wives from the land of Canaan. His wives were Adah, Elon the Hittite's daughter and Oholibamah, Anah's daughter who was Zibeon the Hivite's granddaughter.

ref: That's why Esau made his home in the hill country of Seir.

text: Wa suataŋa yaa weeni ayen, <<Fi a yaali ayen fi a dɛ naam tama zuk kama? Fi le sum a nya tama zuwa? Ate ba yaa ŋmasi kisi wa yega wa gɔdaasimaŋa nyiŋ ale wa le weeni diila nyiŋ.

translated: Israel said to Joseph, "Are your brothers not herding the sheep in Shechem? Come, I will send you to them."

ref: Joseph answered, “I'm looking for my brothers who are watching the sheep. Can you tell me where they are?”
text: Wa yaa weeni wa ayen, <<Mi a gisi ka m suataŋa. Fi baga a dak ba le ta ba piisaŋa a bo juijui ate ŋa a dɛ la?>>
translated: If you see your friend, <<I have heard about my problem. Why don't you go and tell them to come quickly so that we can discuss it?>>

ref: All of Jacob's children came to comfort him, but he refused to be comforted. “No,” he said, “I will go to my grave, mourning for my son.” So Jacob kept on grieving.
text: Wa nidoabilisaŋa ale wa liema a jam ayen ba yik wa ate wa sui yɔk, alege wa zɛri. Wa weeni ayen, <<Aaya, mi a kum kama ayen m ga siŋ vɔrup a cheŋ m biika jigi.>> Dila wa kowa a yaa kum wa biika.
translated: His wife agreed and took the meat and cooked it for her husband to eat, and he ate it. She asked him, "Father, have you ever eaten meat as sweet as my child's flesh?" Then she went and buried her child.

ref: But Er was very evil, and the Lord took his life.

ref: The next morning the king was upset. So he called in his magicians and wise men and told them what he had dreamed. None of them could tell him what the dreams meant.

translated: If you refuse to let them go and continue to hold them back, the hand of the LORD will bring a terrible plague on your livestock in the field - on your horses, donkeys, camels, cattle, sheep and goats. Pharaoh will see that the LORD will make a distinction between the livestock of Israel and those of Egypt, so that no animal belonging to the Israelites will die.

ref: and everything happened just as he said it would. I got my job back, and the cook was put to death.

translated: But if you do not forgive people their sins, your Father will not forgive your sins either. My people, this is what I want to tell you, but the time has come for us to end today's message.

ref: “Your Majesty,” Joseph answered, “I can't do it myself, but God can give a good meaning to your dreams.”
